problem_statement: AI coding agents accelerate code generation but introduce redundancy (Type-4 semantic clones) that traditional metrics and human reviewers fail to catch, and existing benchmarks only measure pass rates rather than long-term maintainability. It examines whether reviewer sentiment detects these quality issues in AI-generated pull requests.
magnitude_evidence: Average Max Redundancy (AMR) for AI agents is 0.2867, compared to only 0.1532 for humans, representing a nearly 1.87x increase (Mann-Whitney p < 0.001)
insufficiency_evidence: reviewers express fewer negative emotions towards Agentic-PRs than Human-PRs despite higher redundancy; 'reviewers may lower their vigilance'; 'existing tools are not effective at detecting these Type-4 semantic clones'
solution_mechanisms: reviewer sentiment analysis of review comments
